global_cues.us_markets: Mixed close on Apr 29 after Fed left rates unchanged and Trump announced US blockade of Iranian ports: S&P 500 -0.04% to 7,135.95; Nasdaq +0.04% to 24,673.24; Dow -0.57% to 48,861.81 as oil surged. Big Tech (Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta) reported after the bell — overnight reactions will set tone for IT names like TCS, INFY, HCLTECH.
global_cues.european_markets: Modest losses on Apr 29 — DAX -0.27% to 23,954.56 weighed by rising oil and Fed caution. Broader Stoxx 600 mixed; energy outperformed on Brent surge.
global_cues.asian_markets: Asia trading cautiously this morning amid Brent crude spike to ~$120/bbl and Trump Iran blockade headline. Nikkei/Hang Seng under pressure on tech wobble after OpenAI revenue miss earlier in week. GIFT Nifty had signalled positive open on Apr 29 (+50 pts) but follow-through likely capped by overnight oil shock and Big Tech earnings reaction.

fii_dii_summary: FIIs net SELLERS at -₹2,468 Cr on Apr 29; DIIs net BUYERS at +₹2,262 Cr. DII absorption continues to cushion the tape but FII outflows persist amid record-low INR (~94.85) and elevated crude — net flow trend remains negative for foreign capital in the Iran-war regime.
earnings_calendar: HINDUNILVR (Hindustan Unilever) - Apr 30 (Q4 FY26 + final dividend; FMCG bellwether)
ADANIPORTS (Adani Ports & SEZ) - Apr 30 (Q4 FY26 + dividend; concall 6pm IST; consensus +21% PAT YoY to ₹3,054 Cr, +22% rev to ₹9,705 Cr per pre-release indications)
ADANIENT (Adani Enterprises) - Apr 30 (Q4 FY26 + dividend + potential fund-raise approval)
NATIONALUM (National Aluminium) - Apr 30 (Q4 FY26; metals read-through given freight cost surge)
CENTRALBK (Central Bank of India) - Apr 30 (Q4 FY26; PSU bank)
~47 NSE/BSE companies reporting on Apr 30 alone
macro_events: US FOMC held rates unchanged on Apr 29; statement language and Powell presser interpreted as cautious amid oil shock — supportive for EM rates but USD strength persists
Trump announced US blockade of Iranian ports on Apr 29, escalating West Asia conflict that began Feb 28 — Strait of Hormuz disruption ongoing
RBI April MPC (Apr 6-8) held repo at 5.25% with neutral stance; FY27 GDP cut to 6.9% (from 7.4%) and FY27 CPI projected at 4.6% — next MPC Jun 3-5
Moody's cut India FY26-27 GDP forecast to 6% (from 6.8%); Goldman Sachs cut CY2026 to 5.9% (from 7%)
Indian crude basket above $100/bbl vs ~$60 throughout FY26 — every $10 oil rise adds 20-25bps to inflation
Big 4 US Big Tech earnings (GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, META) released after Apr 29 close — overnight prints drive sentiment for INFY, TCS, HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM, LTIM
sector_themes: Energy/Oil & Gas: Brent +8.13% to ~$120 on Trump Iran blockade — upstream beneficiaries ONGC, OIL, RELIANCE; downstream OMCs IOC, BPCL, HPCL face margin compression. ATGL reported Apr 27

Banking: Bandhan Bank (BANDHANBNK) +11% on Apr 29 after Q4 PAT +68% YoY to ₹534 Cr — positive read for mid-tier private banks. PSU banks like CENTRALBK, PNB, BANKBARODA, CANBK, UNIONBANK in focus on Q4 prints amid RBI ECL framework

IT services: TCS workforce shrunk ~24,000 in FY26; INFY headcount -8,440 in Q4FY26; FY27 fresher hiring guided at 25,000 (TCS) and ~20,000 (INFY) vs 40-42k historical. AI-driven automation pressuring margins; INFY, TCS, HCLTECH, WIPRO, TECHM under structural overhang

Metals/Steel: Capesize freight rates up from $9.80 to $12.20/tonne on Strait of Hormuz disruption — raises landed cost of met coal & iron ore for TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, JINDALSTEL, SAIL. NATIONALUM reports today
Fertilizers/Chemicals: Urea/fertilizer plants cut output >40% on Qatar LNG supply curtailment — negative for CHAMBLFERT, GNFC, RCF, NFL; LNG-linked GAIL, PLNG also affected
FMCG: HINDUNILVR Q4 today is a key tape-setter for rural recovery thesis; rupee weakness pressures input costs (palm oil, crude derivatives)
Infrastructure/Ports: ADANIPORTS Q4 today expected strong (+21% PAT/+22% rev YoY); Adani group complex (ADANIENT, ADANIPOWER, ATGL, ADANIGREEN) in focus
Auto: Earnings season ramps next week with MARUTI; rupee/oil pressure margins, but rural demand thesis supportive — TVSMOTOR, M&M, HEROMOTOCO, BAJAJ-AUTO read-throughs
risk_flags: USD/INR at all-time low ~94.85 (touched 94.80 intraday on Apr 29) despite RBI intervention — rupee depreciation imports inflation and pressures FII flows
Brent crude at $118-120/bbl (+8.13% on Apr 29) on Trump Iran blockade — every $10 adds 20-25bps to India CPI; widens current account deficit
West Asia conflict (US/Israel vs Iran since Feb 28) — Strait of Hormuz partially closed, India imports 85% of crude / 60% LPG / 50% LNG via this route
220,000+ Indian nationals repatriated from Gulf/Iran — remittance flows at risk (Gulf = >1/3 of India remittances) with downstream pressure on Kerala/AP banks and consumption
FII selling persists (-₹2,468 Cr on Apr 29) with cumulative FY27 outflows weighing on largecaps — DII buying at +₹2,262 Cr only partially offsets
Big Tech earnings overnight (GOOGL/MSFT/AMZN/META) — negative surprise would compound IT services drag on TCS, INFY, HCLTECH
GDP downgrades stack up (Moody's 6%, Goldman 5.9% for CY26) — earnings revision cycle still skewed downward for cyclicals
brief_summary: Risk-off tilt likely at open: Brent surge to ~$120 on Trump's Iran port blockade plus a record-low rupee (94.85) tighten the macro screw, while US Big Tech post-bell prints drive overnight IT sentiment. Earnings flow is heavy — HINDUNILVR, ADANIPORTS, ADANIENT, BAJAJFINSV are today's tape-setters — and FIIs remain net sellers (-₹2,468 Cr) with DIIs (+₹2,262 Cr) only partially absorbing. Trade defensively into the open: favor energy upstream (ONGC, OIL) and reporting bellwethers with positive consensus over OMCs, IT services, and rate-sensitive financials.
